keyphrases.appears_in: In Java, such a group of objects manipulated as a single entity is called a collection ◮ Java language environment (JDK) provides a Collections Framework that hold together several classes and interfaces of the type collection ◮ Quiz: are you able to differentiate between Java, JVM and JDK?

Collections vs ADTs ► There are familiar composite data structures behaving as collections called Abstract Data Types (ADT) Main among these are the List, Stack (LIFO), Queue (FIFO), Tree, and Table ►

In the Java Collections Framework, there are 2 main root interface classes: 1.

◮ Consistent API: The API has a basic set of interfaces (Collection, Set, List, Queue, Deque, Map, etc) and a set of classes that implement these interfaces (HashSet, TreeSet, ArrayList, LinkedList, ArrayDeque, HashMap, TreeMap, Vector, etc) ◮ All the implementation classes have some common set of methods ◮ Reduced programming effort: Due to the use of a common set of conceptually abstract methods, programmers can effectively use the OOP concepts of Abstraction and Encapsulation ◮ Increased program performance: Availability of high-performance implementations of essential data structures and algorithms improves both memory use and run-time efficiency

The threshold at which rehashing happens along with capacity increase is computed as Capacity x Load Factor and has an initial default value of 12 ◮ Rehashing is the process of doubling the capacity of the HashMap after it reaches its threshold ◮ Quiz: what is the default sequence of rehashing sizes for HashMap?
The fraction of buckets used after which the capacity is increased is the load factor ◮
